html:
“After being planted on the ground, it will trigger when a creature walks near.

It needs to be reset after triggering. It can be picked up and moved.”

-

A Tooth Trap is a craftable Weapon that can be placed on the ground and deals 60 damage to any Mob that steps on it. It has 10 uses and must be reset after each use. Tooth Traps are found in the Fight Tab, require a Log, a Rope and a Hound's Tooth to craft, and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.

Tooth Trap damage will not aggro nearby mobs, nor will it increase a player's Naughtiness. Tooth Traps are also fireproof in all versions of the game. A Tooth Trap can be picked up and placed elsewhere at any time, regardless of having been triggered or not.

Contents

  • 1 Mobs
  • 2 Skins
  • 3 Tips
  • 4 Trivia
  • 5 Assets
  • 6 Gallery

Mobs

The following Mobs cannot activate Tooth and Bramble Traps:

  • Abigail
  • Bee
  • Killer Bee
  • Grumble Bee
  • Bee Queen
  • Butterfly
  • Batilisk
  • Fruit Fly
  • Lord of the Fruit Flies
  • Friendly Fruit Fly
  • Mosquito
  • Birds
  • Chester
  • Hutch
  • Lureplant
  • Dragonfly
  • Glommer
  • Beefalo with Saddle
  • Shadow Creature
  • Shadow Pieces
  • Shriek

Skins

Main article: Belongings

  • The Loyal Rose Thorn Trap skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from Jan 23, 2025 to Feb 20, 2025.
Woven - Elegant
Year of the Pig King Collection
Tiger Fang Trap
No tigers were harmed in the making of this trap. But some humans were.
See ingame
Woven - Elegant
Rose Collection
Rose Thorn Trap
Trap any enemies who stop to smell the roses.

Tips

  • Tooth Traps are ineffective against Lureplants and their Eyeplants; Eyeplants will eat Tooth Traps.
    • Placing man-made Turf will prevent Eyeplants from spawning.
  • A useful way to kill Pigs, or other mobs, without increasing Naughtiness, is to place food near the Tooth Traps (ideally so the trap will kill the victim before it reaches the food).
  • Surrounding small groups of about 4 Berry Bushes with Walls and Tooth Traps allow farming Gobblers, which also protects unharvested Berries from them. When the Gobbler tries to exit, one of the Tooth Traps will kill it instantly.
    • Alternatively, 1 Tooth Trap can be placed in front of a Berry Bush, instantly killing a Gobbler that spawns from the bush.
  • Tooth Traps can be an effective way to kill Boss Monsters, or other tough enemies, if they are densely packed into one area.
  • A "panic room" can be made with as little as 10 Tooth Traps. It should be made of Stone Walls, with as few entrances as possible; more Tooth Traps should be placed near the entrance, as opposed to evenly spread throughout the room. A panic room can be effective against Hound attacks.
  • In Don't Starve Together, players' Ghosts can reset Tooth Traps.

Trivia

  • The Tooth Trap used to cost 100 points under the old Research Points system.
  • Tooth Traps were first added in the Long Live The Queen! update.

Gallery

  • A "planted" Tooth Trap.
  • A triggered Tooth Trap.
  • Either a triggered Tooth Trap on its last use or a Tooth Trap that has been dropped, but not planted.
  • Wilson standing next to a Tooth Trap
  • Tooth Traps set up to hunt Gobblers.
  • A "panic room" corridor, lined with Tooth Traps.
  • An area dense with Tooth Traps to repel Hounds.
  • Tooth Trap as seen in the poster for the Long Live the Queen! update.
  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.

A Tooth Trap is a craftable Weapon that can be placed on the ground and deals 60 damage to any Mob that steps on it. It has 10 uses and must be reset after each use. Tooth Traps are found in the Fight Tab, require a Log, a Rope and a Hound's Tooth to craft, and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.
Tooth Trap damage will not aggro nearby mobs, nor will it increase a player's Naughtiness. Tooth Traps are also fireproof in all versions of the game. A Tooth Trap can be picked up and placed elsewhere at any time, regardless of having been triggered or not.

Rose Collection
Rose Thorn Trap
Trap any enemies who stop to smell the roses.
Tips[edit | edit source]
Tooth Traps are ineffective against Lureplants and their Eyeplants; Eyeplants will eat Tooth Traps.
Placing man-made Turf will prevent Eyeplants from spawning.
A useful way to kill Pigs, or other mobs, without increasing Naughtiness, is to place food near the Tooth Traps (ideally so the trap will kill the victim before it reaches the food).
Surrounding small groups of about 4 Berry Bushes with Walls and Tooth Traps allow farming Gobblers, which also protects unharvested Berries from them. When the Gobbler tries to exit, one of the Tooth Traps will kill it instantly.
Alternatively, 1 Tooth Trap can be placed in front of a Berry Bush, instantly killing a Gobbler that spawns from the bush.
Tooth Traps can be an effective way to kill Boss Monsters, or other tough enemies, if they are densely packed into one area.
A "panic room" can be made with as little as 10 Tooth Traps. It should be made of Stone Walls, with as few entrances as possible; more Tooth Traps should be placed near the entrance, as opposed to evenly spread throughout the room. A panic room can be effective against Hound attacks.
In Don't Starve Together, players' Ghosts can reset Tooth Traps.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Tooth Trap used to cost 100 points under the old Research Points system.
Tooth Traps were first added in the Long Live The Queen! update.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Bẫy Răng là một Vũ khí chế tạo được, có thể đặt trên mặt đất và gây 60 sát thương cho bất kỳ Mob nào dẫm lên nó. Nó có 10 lần sử dụng và phải được cài đặt lại sau mỗi lần sử dụng. Bẫy Răng được tìm thấy trong Thẻ Chiến đấu, yêu cầu Gỗ, Dây thừng và Răng Chó Săn để chế tạo và Máy Giả kim để tạo nguyên mẫu.

It can be picked up and moved.}} A '''Tooth Trap''' is a craftable [[Weapon]] that can be placed on the ground and deals 60 damage to any [[Mob]] that steps on it. It has 10 uses and must be reset after each use. Tooth Traps are found in the [[Fight Tab]], require a [[Log]], a [[Rope]] and a [[Hound's Tooth]] to craft, and an [[Alchemy Engine]] to prototype. Tooth Trap damage will not aggro nearby mobs, nor will it increase a player's [[Krampus#Naughtiness|Naughtiness]]. Tooth Traps are also fireproof in all versions of the game. A Tooth Trap can be picked up and placed elsewhere at any time, regardless of having been triggered or not. == Mobs == The following [[Mob]]s cannot activate Tooth and Bramble Traps:

== [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == {{Main|Belongings#Weapons Skins|Belongings}} * The '''Loyal Rose Thorn Trap''' skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of ''Don't Starve Together'' on Twitch from Jan 23, 2025 to Feb 20, 2025.
{{SkinInfo |image = Tiger Fang Trap Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Year of the Pig King Collection |name = Tiger Fang Trap |description = No tigers were harmed in the making of this trap. But some humans were. |ingame = Tiger Fang Trap.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Rose Thorn Trap Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Rose Collection |name = Rose Thorn Trap |description = Trap any enemies who stop to smell the roses. }}
== {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * Tooth Traps are ineffective against Lureplants and their Eyeplants; Eyeplants will eat Tooth Traps. **Placing man-made [[Turf]] will prevent Eyeplants from spawning. * A useful way to kill [[Pig]]s, or other mobs, without increasing Naughtiness, is to place food near the Tooth Traps (ideally so the trap will kill the victim before it reaches the food). * Surrounding small groups of about 4 [[Berry Bush]]es with [[Walls]] and Tooth Traps allow farming [[Gobbler]]s, which also protects unharvested [[Berries]] from them. When the Gobbler tries to exit, one of the Tooth Traps will kill it instantly. ** Alternatively, 1 Tooth Trap can be placed in front of a Berry Bush, instantly killing a Gobbler that spawns from the bush. * Tooth Traps can be an effective way to kill [[Boss Monsters]], or other tough enemies, if they are densely packed into one area. * A "panic room" can be made with as little as 10 Tooth Traps. It should be made of [[Stone Wall]]s, with as few entrances as possible; more Tooth Traps should be placed near the entrance, as opposed to evenly spread throughout the room. A panic room can be effective against [[Hound]] attacks. * In ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', players' [[Ghost Characters|Ghosts]] can reset Tooth Traps. == {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * The Tooth Trap used to cost 100 points under the old [[Research Points List|Research Points]] system. *Tooth Traps were first added in the [[Don't Starve/Version History#December 19, 2012 – Long Live the Queen|Long Live The Queen!]] update. == {{Pic32|Research Notes}} Assets == Tooth Trap= Tooth Trap Build.png|Inactive Tooth Trap Active Build.png|Active Tooth Trap Dropped.png|Dropped |-| Tiger Fang Trap= Tiger Fang Trap Build.png|Inactive Tiger Fang Trap Active Build.png|Active Tiger Fang Trap Dropped.png|Dropped |-| Rose Thorn Trap= Rose Thorn Trap Build.png|Inactive Rose Thorn Trap Active Build.png|Active Rose Thorn Trap Dropped.png|Dropped == {{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == Tooth trap planted.png|A "planted" Tooth Trap.
